keyword.value: UN. Senior Focal Point on Crimes against Personnel Serving in Peacekeeping Operations
keyword.showAs: UN. Senior Focal Point on Crimes against Personnel Serving in Peacekeeping Operations

keyword.value: Security Council resolution 2823 (2026) [on safety and security of peacekeepers]
keyword.showAs: Security Council resolution 2823 (2026) [on safety and security of peacekeepers]

step.by: Albania, Algeria, Andorra, Antigua and Barbuda, Argentina, Armenia, Australia, Austria, Azerbaijan, Bahamas (The), Bahrain, Bangladesh, Barbados, Belgium, Belize, Bhutan, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Botswana, Brazil, Brunei Darussalam, Bulgaria, Burundi, Cabo Verde, Cambodia, Canada, Central African Republic, Chad, Chile, China, Colombia, Congo, Costa Rica, Côte d’Ivoire, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Denmark, Djibouti, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Eritrea, Estonia, Ethiopia, Fiji, Finland, France, Gabon, Georgia, Germany, Ghana, Greece, Guatemala, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Guyana, Hungary, Iceland, India, Indonesia, Iraq, Ireland, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Latvia, Lebanon, Liberia, Libya, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Maldives, Malta, Marshall Islands, Mauritius, Mexico, Monaco, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, Namibia, Nepal, Netherlands, New Zealand, Niger, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Norway, Oman, Pakistan, Palau, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Qatar, Republic of Korea, Republic of Moldova, Romania, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, San Marino, Saudi Arabia, Senegal, Serbia, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, South Sudan, Spain, Sri Lanka, Sudan, Sweden, Switzerland, Syrian Arab Republic, Tajikistan, Thailand, Timor-Leste, Togo, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Tunisia, Türkiye, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Ukraine, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, United Republic of Tanzania, Uruguay, Uzbekistan, Vanuatu, Viet Nam, Yemen, Zambia, Zimbabwe and Federated States of Micronesia

p.docTitle: Adopted by the Security Council at its 10180th meeting, on23 June 2026

container.name: Recalling
container.p: Recalling the purposes and principles of the Charter of the United Nations, and reaffirming the primary responsibility of the Security Council under the Charter of the United Nations for the maintenance of international peace and security,

container.name: Recalling
container.p: Recalling its previous relevant resolutions and presidential statements, in particular resolutions 2518 (2020) and 2589 (2021) addressing issues related to peacekeeping operations,

container.name: Reaffirming
container.p: Reaffirming the basic principles of United Nations peacekeeping, such as consent of the parties, impartiality, and non‑use of force, except in self-defence and defence of the mandate,

container.name: Underscoring
container.p: Underscoring the importance of peacekeeping as one of the most effective tools available to the United Nations in the promotion and maintenance of international peace and security in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations,

container.name: Condemning
container.p: Condemning in the strongest terms all attacks directed against United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations and their premises in violation of international law, and underscores that such attacks may constitute war crimes, and calls upon all parties to armed conflict to comply fully with their obligations in that regard,

container.name: Recognizing
container.p: Recognizing the important role host states play in ensuring the safety and security of United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations, and in facilitating the implementation of peacekeeping mandates, and stressing that the primary responsibility for the safety and security of United Nations personnel and assets rests with the host state,

container.name: Emphasizing
container.p: Emphasizing the existing prohibitions against attacks knowingly and intentionally directed against personnel involved in peacekeeping operations, who enjoy protection under international law and relevant UN Security Council resolutions,

container.name: Recognizing
container.p: Recognizing that attacks against United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations undermine the effective implementation of Security Council mandates and threaten the effectiveness of United Nations peacekeeping operations, and their role in the maintenance of international peace and security,

container.name: Noting
container.p: Noting that United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations are deployed in complex political and security environments, expressing grave concern at the recent escalation in the number, scope and sophistication of attacks against United Nations peacekeepers and their facilities, including by armed and terrorist groups, through the use of shelling, improvised explosive devices (IEDs), unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and other advanced and emerging technologies,

container.name: Condemning
container.p: Condemning violations of status-of-forces or status-of-mission agreements by any party to those agreements, recognizing the grave risks such violations can present to the safety and security of United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations, and affirming that the entry of personnel and equipment into the host country, as well as the freedom of movement of mission, in line with mission’s mandate, should not be hindered,

container.name: Expressing
container.p: Expressing concern about the risks posed to United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations also as collateral damage arising from hostilities, and stressing the need for parties to the armed conflict to take comprehensive, targeted and deliberate measures to avoid harming such personnel, in accordance with their obligations under international humanitarian law and to assist in enhancing their safety and security,

container.name: Also expressing
container.p: Also expressing concern regarding challenges in bringing to justice perpetrators of the killing of, and all acts of violence against United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations, and that the rate of prosecution of such crimes has remained very low, which has further contributed to an environment of impunity and undermined the safety and security of such personnel,

container.name: Recognizing
container.p: Recognizing the importance of taking immediate and concrete steps to ensure accountability for attacks against United Nations personnel and their premises,

content.p: Pays tribute to all United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations and those who have sacrificed their lives in the line of duty for the cause of peace;

Stresses that accountability for attacks against United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations is essential to deter future attacks, safeguard the effectiveness of United Nations peacekeeping operations and the safety and security of such personnel, and calls upon all relevant stakeholders, as appropriate, to cooperate with the United Nations to facilitate the identification, investigation and prosecution of perpetrators without delay;

Recalls the responsibility of Host States to work with peacekeeping operations to enhance the safety and security of United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations, including in line with resolution 2518 (2020) and resolution 2589 (2021), and to take all necessary measures to investigate the killing of and all acts of violence against United Nations personnel in peacekeeping operations to prosecute the perpetrators, in line with their national law, in a manner that is consistent with applicable international obligations, including under international humanitarian law, and international human rights law, and in accordance with the status-of-forces or status-of-mission agreements;

Requests the Secretary-General, as appropriate and where mandated, to ensure that in event of any future attacks on personnel serving in peacekeeping operations and their premises, peacekeeping operations promptly establish clear factual records of the incidents and make them available for host states’ investigations into such attacks, calls upon all relevant States and other relevant actors to cooperate fully with such investigations, and to take all appropriate measures to bring perpetrators to justice and requests the Secretary General to keep relevant troop- and police-contributing countries and relevant States informed of progress in investigations and accountability efforts;

Encourages the Secretary-General, through peacekeeping operations, where mandated, and other relevant United Nations entities, within existing resources and upon the request of host state, to continue to provide capacity-building and technical assistance to national law enforcement, prosecutorial, judicial and corrections institutions to support the investigation and prosecution of the killing of, and all acts of violence against United Nations personnel serving or having served in peacekeeping operations;

Encourages troop- and police-contributing countries, whose personnel have been the victims of acts of violence to deploy, on a voluntary basis, as appropriate, trained investigative personnel to assist the host States, consistent with their national law, upon their request, in the investigations of cases related to the killing of, and all acts of violence against United Nations personnel serving or having served in peacekeeping operations;

Requests the Secretary-General to designate a senior focal point, among current personnel, on accountability for crimes against personnel serving in peacekeeping operations, to bolster coordination and the capacity to address these issues across the United Nations system to support the prevention, investigation and prosecution of cases related to the killing of and all acts of violence against United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations, including through engagement with relevant Member States and with troop- and police-contributing States, and to enhance the effective use of existing accountability mechanisms;

Requests the Secretary-General to submit to the Security Council annual report on the status of the investigation and prosecution of cases related to the killing of and all acts of violence against United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations. The report should include information on the work carried out by the Secretariat and the focal points appointed in each peacekeeping operation, as applicable. The first such report should be submitted within 120 days of the adoption of the resolution. It should contain a list of options on the way forward for consideration by the Council;

Requests the Secretary-General to include, as an annex to his above-mentioned report to the Security Council, any annual updates provided by Member States hosting or having hosted United Nations peacekeeping operations on progress made in the investigation and prosecution of cases related to the killing of, and all acts of violence against United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations;

Reaffirms its determination to take further steps, as necessary, to strengthen the accountability for attacks on United Nations personnel serving in peacekeeping operations, to enhance their safety and security;
Decides to remain seized of the matter.
